notes: Single direct dep: root -> my-portal-pkg (local/portal:, editable=true). No transitive deps.
portal: is a yarn-compat symlink protocol. Install behavior is identical to link: (symlink, no copy, no install hooks).
The only observable difference from link: in the dep tree is the protocol token in source_detail.protocol ('portal:' vs 'link:').
editable=true because portal: creates a symlink — edits to vendor/my-portal-pkg reflect immediately without reinstall.
If Mend maps portal: to file: instead, editable would be false — wrong. If Mend maps portal: to registry, resolution would fail — wrong.
This probe has zero transitive deps. If Mend emits any transitive, that is a ghost-dep failure.

local_source_edges.name: my-portal-pkg
local_source_edges.install_behavior: symlink — Bun creates a symlink in node_modules pointing to vendor/my-portal-pkg. Changes to vendor/my-portal-pkg reflect immediately without reinstall. No install hooks are run (unlike file: which can trigger postinstall). This is the defining characteristic of the portal: protocol.

protocol_comparison.note: All three local-source protocols (file:, link:, portal:) map to source='local'. They are distinguished only by protocol token and editable flag.

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Bun has no entry in Mend's install-tool list and does not appear in the UA javascript.md resolver file. Detection relies entirely on static bun.lock parsing (text JSONC, Bun 1.2+ format).
portal: MUST map to source='local'. If Mend does not recognize the portal: token, my-portal-pkg will be dropped entirely (most likely failure mode).
portal: is distinct from file: (editable=false, copy) — if Mend classifies portal: as file:, the editable flag will be false instead of true.

portal: and link: share the same install behavior (symlink, editable=true) but use different protocol tokens. A parser that conflates the two will still produce the correct editable flag but will lose the protocol identity in source_detail.protocol. The comparator must assert protocol='portal:' explicitly.
portal: is NOT the same as the npm 'portal' pattern or any npm-native feature. It originates from Yarn and is adopted by Bun for yarn-compat. An npm-resolver fallback will not understand it.

The lockfile workspaces section records portal: with the full protocol token ('portal:./vendor/my-portal-pkg'). A Mend parser that only reads the packages object will drop my-portal-pkg entirely — it appears only in workspaces, not in packages.
This probe has no transitive registry deps (my-portal-pkg/package.json declares empty dependencies). If Mend emits any transitive dep, that is a ghost-dep false positive.

Because the UA javascript.md resolver file has zero mentions of Bun or the portal: protocol, this probe is exploratory (not regression-bound against a documented Mend behavior). A passing result indicates Mend added Bun support; a failing result documents the gap for a Jira bug.
